audit_status.source_base: Greek Acts text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Saul encounter, Ananias, Damascus witness, escape, Jerusalem acceptance, Aeneas healing, Tabitha raising.

verses.restored: Saul, still breathing threat and murder against the disciples of the Lord, went to the high priest,
and asked from him letters to Damascus for the synagogues, so that if he found any belonging to the Way, both men and women, he might bring them bound to Jerusalem.
As he journeyed, it happened that he drew near to Damascus, and suddenly a light from heaven flashed around him.
Falling upon the ground, he heard a voice saying to him, Saul, Saul, why do you pursue me?
He said, Who are you, Lord? He said, I am Jesus, whom you pursue.
But rise and enter the city, and it will be spoken to you what you must do.
The men traveling with him stood speechless, hearing the voice but beholding no one.
Saul rose from the ground, and though his eyes were opened, he saw nothing. Leading him by the hand, they brought him into Damascus.
He was three days not seeing, and he neither ate nor drank.
There was a certain disciple in Damascus named Ananias. The Lord said to him in a vision, Ananias. He said, Look, I am here, Lord.
The Lord said to him, Rise and go to the street called Straight, and seek in the house of Judas a man from Tarsus named Saul; for look, he is praying.
He has seen a man named Ananias coming in and laying hands on him so that he might see again.
Ananias answered, Lord, I have heard from many about this man, how many harmful things he did to your holy ones in Jerusalem.
And here he has authority from the chief priests to bind all who call upon your name.
But the Lord said to him, Go, because this one is a chosen vessel to me, to carry my name before nations and kings and children of Israel.
For I will show him how much it is necessary for him to suffer for my name.

Ananias went away and entered the house; laying hands on him, he said, Brother Saul, the Lord Jesus, the one who appeared to you on the road by which you came, has sent me so that you may see again and be filled with holy Spirit.
Immediately something like scales fell from his eyes, and he saw again. Rising, he was baptized,
and taking food, he was strengthened. He was with the disciples in Damascus some days.
Immediately in the synagogues he proclaimed Jesus, that this one is the Son of the Divine.
All who heard were amazed and said, Is this not the one who ravaged those calling on this name in Jerusalem, and came here for this purpose, to bring them bound to the chief priests?
But Saul was being empowered more and more and confounding the Judeans dwelling in Damascus, proving that this one is the Messiah.
When many days were fulfilled, the Judeans took counsel to kill him.
Their plot became known to Saul. They were also watching the gates day and night so that they might kill him.
But his disciples, taking him by night, let him down through the wall, lowering him in a basket.
Arriving in Jerusalem, he was attempting to join the disciples, and all were afraid of him, not trusting that he was a disciple.
But Barnabas took him, brought him to the apostles, and described to them how on the road he saw the Lord, that he spoke to him, and how in Damascus he spoke boldly in the name of Jesus.
He was with them, going in and out in Jerusalem, speaking boldly in the name of the Lord.
He was speaking and disputing with the Hellenists, but they were attempting to kill him.
The siblings, learning this, brought him down to Caesarea and sent him away to Tarsus.
Therefore the assembly throughout all Judea and Galilee and Samaria had peace, being built up; walking in the reverence of the Lord and in the comfort/encouragement of the holy Spirit, it multiplied.
It happened that Peter, passing through all, came down also to the holy ones dwelling in Lydda.
There he found a certain man named Aeneas, who had been lying on a mat eight years and was paralyzed.
Peter said to him, Aeneas, Jesus Messiah heals you; rise and make your bed. Immediately he rose.
All those dwelling in Lydda and Sharon saw him, and they turned to the Lord.
In Joppa there was a certain disciple named Tabitha, which translated is called Dorcas. She was full of good works and acts of mercy that she was doing.
It happened in those days that she became weak and died. Washing her, they laid her in an upper room.
Since Lydda was near Joppa, the disciples, hearing that Peter was there, sent two men to him, urging, Do not delay coming to us.

Peter rose and went with them. When he arrived, they brought him into the upper room, and all the widows stood beside him weeping and showing tunics and garments that Dorcas made while she was with them.
Peter put them all outside, knelt down, and prayed. Turning to the body, he said, Tabitha, rise. She opened her eyes, and seeing Peter, sat up.
Giving her his hand, he raised her; calling the holy ones and widows, he presented her alive.
It became known throughout all Joppa, and many trusted in the Lord.
It happened that he remained many days in Joppa with a certain Simon, a tanner.
